name: Marshall, Northone Hacot
motto: "Motto: Someone very truthfully made the above remark many years ago, and considering Marshall's character we may very appropriately apply it to him. Lending a helping hand or a bit of encouragement seems to be his chief delight. Who would have thought when 'Marshall' answered the call for spring track that he really intended to run? However, four years of perseverance, backed by his 'Do or die' spirit, have given him a place on the varsity squad, in addition to his captaincy of the cross-country team. Those who knew him loved his spirit. We may truthfully exclaim in the words of Shakespeare: 'He was so gentle, and the element is his to mingle, That nature might stand up and say to all the world, He was a sun.'"

name: Canada, Thomas Nolan
description: Behold the finished product. It is he with the silver tongue, J. Nolan. His desire has not been for scholastic honors but for information concerning that mysterious clan termed "Females." In search of said information he has developed his natural resources, and thus we present him. Who would not believe him when he gazes upon them with his winning smile and offers in his soothing voice such tempting treasures as miniatures and sponsorships? But even with all these accomplishments Nolan is one on whom we can depend. He is a quick thinker and little time is lost waiting for his decision. This has, no doubt, been developed by his athletic career, for he is a good tennis and basketball player and has been a mainstay in that bloody sport known as "Company Football." Nolan is a gentleman, he is a man, and he is a friend.

name: Crockett, Paul Fulton
motto: "Wherever he is his fondest thought And a simple truth his almost skill."
description: A true friend, a loyal "skipper," and a jolly good fellow is "Paul." His home is in Hampton as anyone may ascertain from any of the young ladies from that section; indeed, from his frequent trips to Roanoke it would seem that he is more than mildly interested in the fairer sex. "Paul" has acquired the habit of concentration to such an extent that he does not have to be a grind in order to receive good grades; in fact, it may be truly said that his studies do not interfere with his college activities. It is well known that "Paul's" highest ambition is to become a Civil Engineer and thus far he has more than succeeded. In the outside world, his perseverance, unfailing good nature and winning personality will necessarily carry him far toward achieving his goal.

name: Robert, Disney
motto: "We will sing one song for the old Kentucky home."
description: Everyone knows that Kentucky is noted for its Blue Grass, fast horses, and beautiful women, but that is not all. "Bob" hails from that state, but he only talks about the Blue Grass and the fast horses. His two hobbies are working problems for Electrical Juniors and going hunting; consequently he doesn't have much time for studying but he manages to make good grades in all of his classes. "Bob" is just the kind of a fellow that one cannot help liking and respecting. He is a happy-go-lucky chap and never lets his work interfere with his pleasure. Being a true technician, we predict for him a brilliant success in the electrical world. Men cannot fail to recognize the qualities that Robert possesses, and in the years to come we know that he will hold the same place in the hearts of his fellowmen that he now holds in the hearts of his classmates.

name: Ellison, George Hadley Jr.
motto: "When I open my mouth let no dog bark." - SHAKESPEARE
description: With all due respect to others, "Hock" is about the rarest human we have ever seen. His accomplishments are numerous. As a smoker of cigars he reigns supreme, not even Siegfried in his prime could hold a candle to George. "Hock" can smoke a cigar, pipe, or cigarette; discourses on airplanes, athletics, girls, murder trials, parties, military, and law all in one breath, and really teach you a great deal about each. Hence one can readily understand why he has polled a unanimous vote for three successive years as Tech's biggest "bull slinger." We almost forgot to add that George H. Ellison is perfectly behind the bat as first string catcher. Yet he finds time to look out for all the boys. We are sorry to see you go George, but we hope your stay here is an indication of your future.

name: Haden, Curtis Allen Jr.
description: Early in the fall of '23 there came out of the County of Pendleton, another farmer seeking an education. He has sought and he has found. Here we have Allen Haden, of Covington, Virginia, universally known as "AL." "AL's" career at V. P. I. has been a successful one. First and last he has brought out the good points of his chosen course, Horticulture, and in between times he has managed to take an active part in all branches of student activities. As to "AL's" future life, we predict that it will be successful. If he can freeze the business men of the world as easily as he does the professors on monthly tests, there is no doubt that he will reach the pinnacle of success. Some one said that it is the last punch that breaks the back of failure; "AL" has this punch, better we wish him the success in life that he deserves.

name: Hargrove, John Winston
motto: "STUDENT"
description: "Here comes Johnny; have a seat and we will hear some jokes." Without a doubt Johnny is a wonder; he can play more in a minute than most of us can in an hour, but that is not all; from the looks of his grades he can accomplish more in a year than most of us. After spending two years in the Band, Johnny became rather tired of belonging to the disconcerting noises of the "Highly Tighted" and decided to take military life for a change, thereby adding greatly to the prestige and dignity of "E" Company. Our association with Johnny is one we are proud of. He is ready at any time to lend a helping hand to the other fellows, and his good nature has won for him a warm place in the hearts of his classmates. "Here's luck to you, Johnny! May your future years be as successful as those spent at Tech!"

motto: "Mr. Hodges, if you will stop reading that letter the class will begin."
description: "Dud" is one of the boys who is always remembered by "someone" away, yet he is always there when V.P.I. or his studies are concerned. "Dud" chose Chemical Engineering as his course of study and he is one of the few who have managed to stick to his first choice for four years. He has reached his goal in that almost unbelievable combination of love and studies. "Dud" is blessed with a pleasing personality which has won him many friends while at Tech. We hate to lose "Dud," but what we lose the great world gains. He will certainly be missed by his host of friends when he leaves, for there is no one to take his place in the hearts of his Tech friends.

name: Hutcheson, Richard Maylon
motto: "High ambitions and deeds which surpass it, few overrunning them all."
description: "Hutch" is a name that can be heard on the campus most any time, for when he is not in the physics laboratory correcting papers, he is on the go. When he studies we do not know, but we do know that he is among the writers in his classes. He is a fellow of remarkable ability; besides holding all the offices that were bestowed upon him, he also became assistant instructor in physics. Despite all he has to do, if one only looks in the mail bag daily, it is evident that he gives some of his time to the higher sex. "Hutch" is one of those good-natured chaps, whose pleasing personality has won for him innumerable friends in Blacksburg and the vicinity. With these attributes and many others, we all predict for "Hutch" a future of fame and glory.

name: Kunkel, Joseph McCutchean
clubs: Executive Committee A. I. E. E.
description: "Hey, old lady, any mail for me to-day? No! I wonder why I haven't gotten that letter." Thus began "Joe's" first year at Tech. "Joe" has proven himself to be a tennis player of no mean ability, and has played his team through two very successful seasons. Tennis is his forte but he takes a great interest in all other athletic and student activities. "Joe" has done well in all of his work at V. P. L., and it is this quality that will bring him success in the future. Although not a gay dog, he does not neglect the social side of life. For three years his interest has centered at different points throughout the South but at present he seems to be most interested in letters that come from the capitol city. "Joe's" admirable qualities brought to him a host of friends and we wish him the best of success.

name: Lewis, Richard Rutledge
description: Behold: A versatile youth, who finds Robinson Crusoe a man of varied accomplishments. Adventure, romance, and reality play equal parts in his life. He can become that after seeing "The Midshipman" (it was hard for him to resist the adventure offered by Uncle Sam in a great career). His romantic nature makes for him the pen, the theater, and the moonlight summer nights objects of ultimate poetic delight. His well-balanced personality, good judgment and thoughtfulness, however, have saved him for reality, in which realm he merely tends to pieces of radio, games, and sports. It is hard to part with him, but consolation lies in the belief that he will build from the pieces of the dismantled machines something worthwhile for himself and others.

name: McDonald, John Early
description: What is said of most Scotchmen is not true of "Mac," for we know of no more liberal-hearted man than he. "Mac" is a true friend to us all; loyal to Tech in every way. He is blessed with a magnetic personality, good judgment, lots of wit, and an abundance of energy. He always looks on the bright side of life; never grumbling, always cheerful. For the past two years we have seen "Mac" only on Wednesdays, for he is in Staunton the rest of the time. We often wonder how he does it, but he has been successful in both love and studies. Tech will not be the same when "Mac" leaves, for he will be missed as only a true classmate and friend can be missed. We wish him the success in the future that he has had in the past, and it is with true regret that we bid him goodbye.

name: Noell, William Alfred
clubs: joke editor, the Tech and the Bulwark
toastmaster, every banquet that he attends
description: "Tags—Tags!" The expression may some day be associated with Mr. Webster, but to us it will always recall the originality and spontaneous wit of "AL," who calls words to suit his thoughts, loves to suit the girl and does to suit himself. Many times during the past four years, when care of classes depressed us, and life seemed like a dull drag, we gathered in "AL's" room, and in an atmosphere of enlightenment, the session would progress, and his thoughts were life behind. "AL's" commendable, carefree attitude has made him the popular Captain of his company, a leader in company athletics, joke editor of the Tech and the Bulwark, a member of the Minstrels, and the toastmaster of every banquet that he attends.

name: Rice, James Edward Jr.
description: Lynchburg contributed; Tech accepted; and "Ed" became a cadet. "Ed's" military ambitions were realized when he was appointed a Sergeant and later a Lieutenant. These two positions he filled with ability and honor. But let us not forget that "Ed" was known to his comrades as an exemplar of honesty and integrity. Men are often remembered by the evil they do, while the good perishes with them; but if this criticism were accepted in "Ed's" case, his name would perish and disappear like dew before the morning sun. We may justly say of him: "He lived a life in a world among men." His thoughts were as pure as gold. His heart was as white as the snow that has been; But his looks were never so cold.

name: Turner, Reginald Eisner
motto: "Laugh and the world laughs with you. Weep and you weep alone."
description: "Who is that military looking freshman?" 'Tis none other than "Reggie" Turner, from John Marshall. Richmond has been proud of her native son during his sojourn at Tech, for he has truly proven himself a man. His words fall with the weight of sound judgment and keen advice. Being of a jolly and companionable disposition, "Reggie's" friends are numbered by the score. Our song would be incomplete without a word about the "fair sex." Since camp, "Reggie" has purchased stock in the Post Office Department and is now regularly receiving letters from Washington. Keep up the good work, "Reggie," and if we judge the future by the past your success is assured.

name: Tusing, Joseph Milton
description: He answers to any of these in a voice that would be ample to command a division. It is rumored that the Endless Caverns have lost trade since "Kelly" gave up his little red uniform and position of guide, to wear the blue and gray. But Tech has been the gainer thereof, for the Virginia Tech Minstrels would have been incomplete without him. "Joe" and his ex-old lady, "Ginny" Williams, really put V. P. I. on the map last summer at Camp Meade. When "Joe" leaves Tech, he will be a living example of the student who accomplished much in the academic line and got the most out of American college life. P. S. - He is the only cadet who ever spent four years in the Corps and never wore his cap straight a single time.

name: Vaughan, Conan Wallace
motto: "None knew him but to love him, None named him but to praise."
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Editor Virginia Tech.
Advisory Board Y. M. C. A.
description: Conan started as a platoon leader with no more idea what it was all about than the rest of us. We do not know whether he put in his application for a corporal or not, but we do know that he was one, and a "dillinger" one at that. "Norfolk," by which he is generally known, was placed as guide on the front of the company, probably to aid us in a hasty retreat from "pop's growler," for he did set one mean stride. And now we come to his senior year. To those who know him he is the same congenial friend that began the struggle with us. Versatility seems to be his motto. If your stay at Tech is an indication of your future, Conan, we need never to have any doubt about reaching the top rung on any ladder you attempt to climb.

name: Wahrmann, Harry
motto: "Hey, Rat! What's your name and where are you going?"
description: Such was Harry's introduction to V.P.I. Since then four long years have passed and we have that same Harry as an anguished soldier. In his classes he has been called a scrib, but those who know him are aware that he is not a grind but a jolly good fellow with a heart for any fate. Harry is going to show the world that V.P.I. puts out good electrical engineers and we are sure that he will make good. Whenever there is a tangle to unwind, a problem to work, the chances are he will do it. Last but not least, Harry is not a womanizer but the fair sex never worry him very much. Therefore, we venture to say that his future life will be as successful as he has been his past.

name: Young, John William
description: Did you ever hear of an inventive genius? Well, there is a real one in this class of '27. "J.W." can invent more foolish ideas in ten minutes than an ordinary reasonable person can in a year. Yet all of his ideas are not so foolish after all. He had an idea that "The Tech" could publish two editions in one day, one of them containing twenty pages—and it did. But he also had an idea that a certain young lady thought him the "one and only" for her—but she didn't. "J.W." not only invents ideas but he usually carries them out, even if it takes half the college to help him put them across. He is a hard worker himself and has the gift of executive ability which enables him to get others to work with him.

name: Allen, James Thomas
description: Whenever the agnostics ask, "Can anything good come out of Radford?" we always point to "Tom" as one of the examples that proves that Radford has often been misjudged. "Tom" decided while in high school that he had the attributes of a good "Hogger" and when he entered Tech he matriculated for the Pre-Law course. After one year in this course, however, he was told by his professors that he was quite proficient in that line and did not need their coaching. Therefore, he changed to Business Administration and has made an enviable record in this new field of study. Unlike most of our "serbians," Tom does not sit in his room "dancing" over his classes. His outside activities are numerous and varied. If his future life is as successful as his college life has been, he is certain to enjoy it to the fullest and end up with a happy home and many automobiles.

name: Anderson, George Claiborne
description: Know to one and all that herein lies the tale of the one and only, "Speck" Anderson. Known to every Techman, young and old, and every citizen of Blackstone, "Speck" winds his way among us with a cheery word for each and everyone. We have known him as a quarterback of no mean ability, and a "man among men." Those who know him not well may surmise that it is beyond his power to be serious. But, oh! It is a mistake and a grievous one. "Speck" can put away his foolishness when the occasion arises. A true friend never dies. Mix a contagious smile, a heart as large as the War Memorial Hall, an abundance of true friendship, a pleasing personality, and you have "Speck." We grieve to see you go, "Speck," but a man of your ability is an asset anywhere.

name: Brown, Harley Higheret
motto: "Well, boys, I believe she loves me," says "Harley," after he has met a girl who is "different." Unlike most fellows, girls do not bother "Harley"; they are here today and gone tomorrow.
description: "Harley" has made a host of friends at Tech. Seldom do we find a man who always looks on the bright side of life. "Harley" is one of the few fellows who can do this. Because of his pleasing disposition he has made others happy beside himself. In military we find "Harley" as one of the best officers in the regiment. He is known for being fair and square with all. But all of his interest is not centered on military, for he is as equally successful in his classes, and his support has been felt by many of the college organizations. If "Harley" goes on in life as he has gone through Tech, we feel sure that nothing can stand between him and success.

name: Cardillo, Joseph Vincent
motto: "Well, just to relieve you of the suspense, I'll tell you; it's 'Joey.'"
description: Joey is one man whose eyes have never dimmed with discouragement because he has always been willing to work hard for what he has received. He has won for himself the name of being a hard worker, a true friend, and above all, a "real fellow." Joey started out strong in military and his continued success is shown by the gold band on his cap. Along with many others, he selected for his life work, Electrical Engineering, and though he is not exactly a "scribe," he has managed to collect a few "A's." We can't accuse him of being a "shirk," but he has managed to keep the thoughts of one fair damsel in his direction. All the harm we wish for you, Joey, is that you will make a success in life as you have made at Tech. Here's to you.

name: Culpepper, Leroy Norman Jr.
motto: "don't let your studies interfere with your education,"
description: Thus "Cullie" we have learned to know Deep Creek from another angle. If he is a typical product we want to know the place better. One of "Cullie's" first ambitions was to be an athlete and help make a name for his Alma Mater. Through hard work he has succeeded. He has been on the wrestling squad since his Freshman year and he won for himself the coveted "V" in his Junior year. Although he sticks close to his motto, which is "don't let your studies interfere with your education," he is a good student and is ever ready to help a needy fellow. His dry wit and good nature have won for him a warm place in the heart of all Techmen and we are sure that he will overcome all of the obstacles that confront him in life, as he has all of his opponents on the mat.

name: Esleock, Karl Augustus
description: "Dick" Esleock is a man of very strong personality. He possesses a combination of the qualities of shrewdness, wit and optimism, which combined with his athletic traits, proclaim him a versatile man. For three years "Dick" wore jersey number 13 and performed at halfback position on the big team. Number 13 did not retard Esleock, but rather was an evil omen to the opposing teams. For Dick crashed through their lines to South Atlantic fame his Junior year. "Dick" is a boxer and Captain of the '27 team. This young chap is a poet, too. He can't write anything at anytime, but with the right inspiration he pours forth verse upon verse of sweet nothings (poetry)—and he has written quite a bit of poetry. Figure it out for yourself!

name: Handy, Edward Malvern
motto: "Behold the man that put Hilton Village on the map at Tech!"
description: "Ed's" popularity and pleasing personality won for him at the very beginning, the Presidency of the Freshman Class. His level-headedness and all around common sense have made him stand out as a leader of men. Playing on the freshman football team, and on the varsity for three years, he stood out as a constant lineman. "Ed" is a pitcher of no mean ability. Tech is proud of him as a good, clean athlete.

name: Keister, John Porter
clubs: Private Company "P" : Blacksburg Club; Horticultural Society
Private Company "P" : Blacksburg Club; Horticultural Society; "Skippers" : Dairy Club
description: Porter entered V. P. I. in the fall of 1919 but on account of trouble with his eyes, could not return to school the next year. In the fall of 1921 Porter again entered V. P. I. with the determination to win that coveted sheepskin. Porter studies for personal gain rather than for high grades. He is a resident of Blacksburg, and much of his time that could be otherwise put on classes is required for outside work. Besides having time to work and attend classes he has always had time to take part in outside activities. He has taken an active part in both the Horticultural Society and the Dairy Club. "Porter" has won many friends by his good humor and fair play; he knows not only how to give a joke but also how to take one. "Porter" is a student in Horticulture and we predict that he will do great things in that field.

name: Manning, John Hudson
motto: "HUDSON"
description: It requires a rare personality in any man to enable him to "have his military" and at the same time make a host of friends. "Hudson" is one of the few that we have known who could do this. He has always mixed with the men that were under his command without being overbearing, and yet in a manner that demanded their admiration and respect. When the appointments were made with "Hudson" as ranking Captain, there was not a man in the corps who was not willing to admit that he deserved it, and to wish him the success that he has attained with it. His ability as a good dancer and as a general "good fellow" has led him to the position of Leader of the Cadilliac Club. Many other school activities have gained by his clear thinking and good leadership. It is expected that these qualities will stay with him and lead him on to success in later life.

"HUDSON"
It requires a rare personality in any man to enable him to "have his military" and at the same time make a host of friends. "Hudson" is one of the few that we have known who could do this. He has always mixed with the men that were under his command without being overbearing, and yet in a manner that demanded their admiration and respect. When the appointments were made with "Hudson" as ranking Captain, there was not a man in the corps who was not willing to admit that he deserved it, and to wish him the success that he has attained with it. His ability as a good dancer and as a general "good fellow" has led him to the position of Leader of the Cadilliac Club. Many other school activities have gained by his clear thinking and good leadership. It is expected that these qualities will stay with him and lead him on to success in later life.

name: Obenshain, Samuel Shockley
description: In writing a biography of "Sam" we find it impossible to do him justice with so few words. If we must say anything against him, it will have to be that he is in love with one girl instead of them all. Yes, his other two faults are going to breakfast and writing eight letters a week to the same girl. "Sam" started off with a long, but after a term he decided to work for Uncle Sam. However, Uncle Sam was unable to hold him, so back to V. P. I. he came. From the start he proved to be a star and took his place with the leaders of our class; not only in class work but also in other activities. Under his leadership the Dairy Club has prospered as never before. If "work is the father of success," we have no doubt of "Sam's" future.

name: Carroll, Richard Shirley
description: "Carroll" entered V. P. I. with the class of '26 but did not return to school the following year. After a vacation of one session he cast his lot with the fair credo of William and Mary, but the life there did not appeal to him as a man's job, so he returned to V. P. I. and entered his junior year with the class of '27. "Carroll" was somewhat handicapped by having two weaknesses, a military ambition and women. For the first, he has been justly rewarded and has made a good officer; for the latter, he is continually increasing in his weakness, for he is a natural favorite among the fairer sex. "Carroll" is a good student and anything he does, he does well. He is a true friend and is the possessor of a very high moral code. He is a good sailor and has won many friends here, as elsewhere. With such qualities could he help from making his mark in life?

name: Weld, Henry Sterling
description: No—another alumnus of the band who has miraculously achieved military success. After serving two years as a high private in the bugle corps, he transferred to "F" company and merited, first, sergeant's chevrons and later Lieutenant's. Thus it is the man and not the environment which makes for success—when thrown suddenly into military life junior year his grin triumphed, and he carried his chevrons as if born to them. And his success in the old, old game of hearts has been even more singular. With the ease and grace of a Dean Brommel he appeared from the ranks of those who would score the fair and has etched the familiar "Sterling" in the memories of "celibate" innumerable. With all, however, he has met those difficult problems which confront the prospective mechanical engineer with a consistency and real determination which have served to send him through four years without falling behind a single hour.

name: Clements, Willis Cunningham
motto: "Such popularity must be deserved."
clubs: Lieutenant Company "Q" (1926-27); Mineral Club; The Union; Bachelor's Club
description: Men are always eager to recognize in a man the qualities present in "Clem's" make-up. Not with men only is the boy popular. Girls are attracted and held by his personality—and, perhaps by what he tells them. What promises more for "Fortune's favorite" than a cheerful friendliness, willingness and ability to carry more than his share, regard for others, and good looks? Such qualities in a miner will claim respect for him when he has moved away from V. P. I. and the men of his class. We'll all look "Clem" up at the big reunion. As a friend the boy from "Dear Richmond" is above par; as an old lady's cousin, he will win; he can't lose.

name: Gallep, Walter Benjamin
hometown: R. F. D. No. 2, Virginia Beach, Va.
description: It was a cold and windy night on the plateau on which is situated the mighty town of Blacksburg, and incidentally that tower of learning known as "Tech." As the ducks of Virginia Beach "waddled" their way on their western course to the land of more and better sunshine, "Ducking" Gallep withdrew himself from that mighty caravan and roosted on this said plateau. Even like his nickname would seem to indicate that his progress on land was slow, contrary to all expectations, he "Galloped" around the bases of the baseball diamond. "Duck" is a "Skipper" in every meaning of the word and his ideals of such a life were never marred by his spontaneous appearance at any militaristic gathering. As a Civil Engineer "Duck" surely likes his field work and is right there with the transit and level. It will not be long before he will be giving his knowledge of structures to the world at large and so build a monument for himself.

name: Mills, Everette Gay, Jr.
description: We introduce to you the only and original flying Lieutenant, Mills E. Gay, Jr., who came into our midst from the city of Suffolk. "Skillet," in his four years' sojourn here, has become one of the most popular members of our class. He is well known by all the ladies, as his circulars in and around Blacksburg testify. He chose to be a "skillet pusher" from the start and has stuck to his transit ever since. The many stakes and studs in the hills and valleys around the campus show us this is true. Not all of his time was spent here, Camp Humphries claimed six weeks of his summer vacation, where his pleasing and learning comeliness gave rise to the nickname of "Mill Day." He is a faithful, loyal, and true friend, who always considers the other fellow first. His many friends wish him all the happiness possible and the greatest success.

name: Arter, Curie Joy
description: The most notable thing about "Delta" is his diminutive stature, from which his nickname is derived; but his thoughts and accomplishments are by no means presented to his size. While he is not rated as one of the soldiers, "Delta" has many managed to get pretty good grades all the way through. It goes without saying, like all seniors, he knew his military, knew it so well that he was finally honored by the highest of all—that of "Skipper." "Delta" is known for and for one of V.P.I.'s best athletes—within the mess hall. It is said that he will challenge any two men regardless of their size, to an eating contest, and allow them the aid of a dog as a handicap. Those who know "Delta" are sure that he will go far in his chosen field. Luck to him!

name: Cato, Lee
motto: "But there is neither East nor West, border, nor breed, nor birth. When two strong men stand face to face, though they come from the ends of the earth."
description: Cato well illustrates the truth in Kipling's great poem by showing us that the gentleman of the East is not different from the gentleman of the West. With his gentlemanly conduct, pleasing personality, and ready wit, he has endeared himself to the hearts of all Technicians. Despite the difficulty of learning a new language, Cato leaves a record which is entirely satisfactory. Realizing that college activities are as important as class work, he displayed his athletic ability as a speedy hurdler on the freshman and varsity track teams and as a valuable tennis player. With his great ambition and high ideals there is no limitation as to what Cato may do for his country and the world in the future.

name: McCalep, Thomas Judson
motto: "To thine own self be true, and it must follow, as the night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man."
description: "Mac" is an electrician—on the professors say, and all the "A's" he gets prove that they are right. Likewise he is a poet—at least the girls say so, and haven't you seen the mail bag rushing to his room after every mail? He is an artist—haven't you heard him sing to his platoon on the drill field? At least you have seen his platoon follow him to mess. This march to the growley ball has been many times interpreted by that old currency: "one two three four, how I wish there were more; (more growley, says "Mac"!) This "Mac" has done, making it just a bit harder for those who have known him to say goodbye and good luck to Tech and to "Mac."

name: Montague, Daniel Junkin
description: After many long and weary years at sea for Uncle Sam, in which 'Dan' upheld the maxim of a sailor, 'a girl in every port,' he landed in shipshape form on the campus of V. P. I. Soon after his arrival, he fell in ranks and answered 'So' instead of 'aye, yee, Sir.' His training on shipboard stood him in good stead and he soon became one of the leaders in the Corps of Cadets. It seems that his numerous pursuits on the South Sea Isles developed him into one of outstanding track men, and to crown his endeavors he was elected captain in this sport. He also captains in another major sport, that of military. Captain 'Dan,' as he's known, will always be remembered as the life of any aggregation, be it a 'ball' or a company, his favorite sport, or his chosen profession.

name: Page, Otis Ware
description: Perhaps you have never met him, but if you have not, the girls may yet smile upon you. In some dark hour when all is dull and you are wandering up and down on the earth in search of adventure—yes; even in the darkest hour, when a pleasant destiny seems denied you, you may have that pleasure and in that moment you shall say, "Kismet—it is fate." But why should this be, you ask? Because when the immortals turned their heads to the shaping of men they sometimes put an extra drop of business in one mould, and when the work was completed, when the clay had hardened and become a man, that man was just a little better than his fellows. Thus, if you should chance upon him in some far corner of the world or in your own back yard, at once your thought will be: "I've met a man."

name: Plummer, Henry Lyne Jr.
description: Back in the days when the hen laid good eggs for a dozen, and the eagle on the half dollar was not afraid to look the Goddess of Liberty in the face; it was then that the name Plummer was found on the list of Freshmen at the Virginia Polytechnic Institute. Henry started with the class of '26 but due to unworkable circumstances he was forced to drop out a year, and has been with us the last three years. Just as soon as Henry saw his first smoke bomb he sought his calling as a chemist. In this line of work he has proved himself to be a very efficient man. This can be seen by the interest he has taken in his work at Tech. When Henry leaves this institution the school will lose a very valuable man, but the loss will be compensated by gaining a very loyal alumnus.

name: Pratt, Thomas Gannaway
description: When Tom came to V. P. L. he thought Aggie was the only course worthy of his consideration, and consequently signed up for a two-year course in that department. When he was a rat he yielded himself to the friendly advantages of the upper classmen, mainly the sophomores, and captivated them on many occasions by his true-to-life vocal imitation of the far-famed mates of the Draper. Such is rat life. The next year was one of work for the privilege of continuing his college education. When he came back to V. P. L. the angle course had been discontinued. On thinking the matter over Tom decided to stick around and delve into the more complicated course of Mechanical Engineering. He is determined to master it. As to his personality and other merits, his associates will tell you that he is right there and really is the life of the party. An ex-voto, Tom.

name: Shepherd, John Murdaugh
description: One might say that John was truly an innocent when we first made his acquaintance. His hometown of Suffolk can certainly say with a clear conscience that he was once a boy and as big a lover of home life as any of his kind. V.P.L. trains one along many lines and that sweet innocence of youth was quickly dispelled, leaving behind, however, its fundamental business. Now John has many and varied duties to fill with the members of the so-called "talented sex." To come back to the serious side of life, the ambition of many years is nearing its fulfillment and John will soon be earning his living in the capacity of an Electrical Engineer. He has the advantage of many—his vocation will also be his hobby. A man will surely win out if his heart is in his work.

name: Garland, Stewart
description: It was on a dark and dreary night that a man was heard down the hall. "Prez, I'm freezing, Old Lady, I freeze!" It was such a cold night that brought forth into the limelight our warm-hearted and faithful friend, "Skinny." In the beginning it seemed that someone enkindled "Skinny" with a germ of militarism, but it did not take. As the years rolled on he proved himself worthy to be knighted a "Skipper." On the occasion of his knighthood his superior said to him, "Arise, brave and noble knight; go forth and subdue the world with your knowledge of inviolate spar yards." Such knowledge, and more besides, is required for a degree in Mechanical Engineering as "Skinny" soon found out, and accordingly he applied himself to his task. With the help of his mentors, he unraveled the many mysteries of his profession and will soon feel confident to face the world of mechanics.

name: Wilburn, William Joseph
description: Curly locks, and electrical engineering occupy respective calls in the mind of our pal, "BILL." Curly locks give him the least trouble; his are too natural to be true, and it is a small wonder that the young lads of this world look upon them with wonder and admiration. So much for his inherent character. Besides, of the other two points of especial interest, cross country is his hobby, and electricity his vocation. His efforts along both of these lines have been persevering and successful; and that is all that can be asked. "BILL" also has had a varied military record, but as the old saying goes, "all is well that ends well," and he emerged at the finish at the head of the platoon. Above all, we remember the never-to-be-forgotten "bull" sessions in which "BILL's" good humor and ready wit played such an important part, and made our college life enjoyable.

name: Alvir, James Kenneth
clubs: Vice President Agricultural Student Honor Council
description: Never in his room, always on the go and known by all his fellow students. This person's name other than "J. K." He is very seldom seen carrying a book to class and furthermore "J. K." does not worry over his books. Nevertheless he has made an enviable record and also many steadfast friends. Among his many requirements is his ability to get where he wants to even when the roads are rough and his trusty three-wheeled vehicle won't always run right side up. Still "J. K." insists that there is nothing like traveling to make a man learned. "J. K." is always on the lookout for something new. We are sure that he will be very successful in any field he might choose. "J. K.," we wish you a successful career and when your success is made, may the "Southwest" open her arms to receive you as she did in our days at Tech.

name: Brawner, James Hugh Jr.
description: From the windows of a real estate dealer's parades rolls "JEMS." He, in fact, comes from the ancient city of Saint Petersburg, in Florida. First in classes, first in great fellowship, and first in the hearts of the fairer sex describes him simply and truthfully. "JEMS" entered Tech in 1923 with a cheerful frame of mind and a full case, and he decided his fate the first day in joining "H" company and enrolling for the course in Chemical Engineering. How he will succeed in his chosen profession may well be left to the future. "JEMS" will soon leave Y. P. L., but he will never leave his place in the memories of his friends and classmates. We wish him luck in life.

description: If you have never become acquainted with "Sam," you surely are the loser. His sterling worth and true man of character win for him the admiration of all those who know him. Different from most of us he has never allowed the members of the fairer sex to cause him much concern; however, we feel that in the near future some girl is sure to capture his heart. "Sam" has chosen as his profession Civil Engineering, and we are sure that he will make good as he has the ability and gift to put things across.

name: Colbert, William Emmett
clubs: Sergeant Company "H"; A. R. C. E. (1924-25)
Private Company "H"; A. R. C. E. (1926-27)
description: Say! You there, from the nation's capital, the city of famous men and powerful politicians, you are not a politician but one of her men. Why, it's William Colbert. (For character study, pictures are attached). To say that our "Bill" is "Bill" is putting it mild; our "Bill" is loved. If you don't believe it, ask her. Don't embrace "Bill" by using the word "worry," because he doesn't know the meaning. A little touchstone, a little institutions, alluring personality, good company, and a go-getter; there now, you have it, a rare combination. As an officer this year "Bill" has been a good private. Once a corporal, once a sergeant, and now a lieutenant but always one for the advancement of better ideals. Whenever "Bill" had any spare moments you would always find him pursuing his course in Civil Engineering. We only hope that the world will need another ditch like the Panama Canal so "Bill" can further prove his quality. A safe bet, ask someone that knows him.
text: Say! You there, from the nation's capital, the city of famous men and powerful politicians, you are not a politician but one of her men. Why, it's William Colbert. (For character study, pictures are attached). To say that our "Bill" is "Bill" is putting it mild; our "Bill" is loved. If you don't believe it, ask her. Don't embrace "Bill" by using the word "worry," because he doesn't know the meaning. A little touchstone, a little institutions, alluring personality, good company, and a go-getter; there now, you have it, a rare combination. As an officer this year "Bill" has been a good private. Once a corporal, once a sergeant, and now a lieutenant but always one for the advancement of better ideals. Whenever "Bill" had any spare moments you would always find him pursuing his course in Civil Engineering. We only hope that the world will need another ditch like the Panama Canal so "Bill" can further prove his quality. A safe bet, ask someone that knows him.

name: Daugherty, William Henry
description: "BILL" came to us from a farm near Franklin, Virginia. He came to V. P. I. with the determination to make the "professors" sit up and take notice of his scholastic abilities; and he has accomplished his purpose. Added to his abilities as a "scribe," he has achieved the rank of Lieutenant in the "Major's Army." Somebody said that "BILL" was a woman-hater; but on the contrary he never misses a date, and is frequently seen rushing the Pastor's daughter. Whatever he does, he does well. With this sterling quality we predict great things for his future. His pleasing personality and winning ways have won him a host of friends, including the opposite sex. In Agronomy he has the highest ambitions. We are sure success awaits you. "BILL": go get it.

clubs: Presiding Officer at the Induction of Miles Stadium
description: When it is considered that the honors conferred upon "Buddy" his Senior year are by popular vote of the Corps and by appointment of authorities of the college, it is clear that "Buddy" has won the esteem of his fellows. For our regard of him "there's a reason." He has won our respect in his years at V.P.I. by cheerfully doing well the things he has done for the betterment of the student body. He has contributed no little part to the spirit of the Corps.

name: Benjamin, Gimbert Gabriel
motto: "Pick your subject, choose your side, and let's have some fun."
description: Yes, that's Gimbert, whose fondness for argument and steadfast refusal to lower, or to depart from his ideals, along with his own personality, has made his sojourn here at Tech a success. Though always ready for a joke, or to help a fellow out of trouble, he has applied himself to his work with a diligence which well merits his success. However, in spite of his good nature and ready smile, there is one thing which causes him a very deep regret. That is, he has never received any quality credits from his "campus course," which he firmly believes should hold at least a minor position in the curriculum. "Bege," old man, we wish you success, and sincerely hope that your pathway will be strewn with flowers, especially "white flowers of the Elizabethan type."

name: Guy, Louis Lee
clubs: Private Company "H": Secretary, Treasurer, German Club
Private Company "H": Parliamentarian, Maury Literary Society
description: Aside from receiving sweet letters, "Louis" has received many honors at Tech. He is the only man in school who has been on the Intercollegiate Debating Team for four years in succession. His ability as a debater has not only won him medals, but has also made him outstanding in the School of Business Administration. Louis expects to continue his education next year by studying law. We feel sure that in his chosen profession he will battle his way to the heights of success.

name: Hicks, Wellington Guy
description: In the picture above you get a glimpse of the pleasant visage of Wellington Guy Hicks, of Rockville, Maryland. Born after his advent to V.P.I., his "Brother Rats" dubbed him with the handle "Duke" because of the historical significance of his unpublicized first name. "Duke" has carried this name with him through his career at Tech. "Duke" is cheerful and fun-loving, and although sleepy is one of his hobbies, he has always managed to keep well up in his work at Poly. He is a diligent worker and goes about his duties in a business-like manner that speaks well for his early training. His successful four years at V.P.I. stand out as a tribute to his unflinching courage, genuine good nature and whole-hearted effort. We are all looking for big things from "Duke," and although we hate to see the big fellow leave Techland, we are comforted in the realization that when we lose "Duke," the world is strengthened by the acquisition of a real man.

name: Kirkman, Albert Gwinn
description: Four years ago, a young man arrived at V. P. I., looked the place over, seemed to like it, and decided to secure living accommodations. He was assigned to Company "H" where he has created an enviable record. As a Freshman he was very quiet but this same man has proved himself to be a man among men. For two years he gave his energy and time to the football squad, gained valuable experience, and then played a great part in helping his company win the regimental championship in 1925. In view of his efforts he was elected captain of "H" Company's 1926 team. The name of the man is Albert Gwinn Kirkman, but is known as "Kirk," a real man, ever ready to help his fellow men, and the kind of a friend that it is an honor to have. Anyone with the character, faithfulness, and dependability of "Kirk" is assured of the best that the world has to offer.

name: Miles, Vernon Edward
description: Let us recall that memorable day in 1924 when the shouts of the class of '27 raised the roof of the old gym as Miles pinned his opponent's shoulders to the mat for the necessary five points to win, as a Freshman, the coveted VP. Thus "Buck" became universally known at Tech. As an athlete, his ability is well known. There was little doubt as to the victorious wrestler when he stepped on the mat. In the football line he can always be depended on. By systematic playing and never-yielding fight, he stops 'em on or usually behind the line. "Buck" is more than this, he is a student of merit. Despite the time devoted to athletics, his classes have not suffered and he has never been in danger of the shipping board. As a true, loyal Techman on the road to success in the big game of life, we will remember him.

name: Schivenor, Arthur Jr.
description: Entering the Band his Freshman year, "Scriv" passed through that trying period of our lives as befits a Freshman. At the end of the year he joined the ranks of "H" Company, and in the three years he has been with us, he has shown himself to be invaluable. As a member of the track team his Freshman and Sophomore years, he proved himself to be invaluable. However, his activities have not been confined to athletics. As director of the Collegians he has been largely responsible for their success as the leading college orchestra of the state. As Athletic Editor of the Tech he leaves behind him a position which will be hard to fill as he did. He also leaves a record that is the envy of all.

name: Wagner, Clifton James
motto: "Where's my letter? Who's got my letter?"
description: This is what we have to listen to three times each day, as "Hans" enters the barracks. Of all in "H" Company he is the most in love, but not in love with the most. He is far beyond redemption, and it seems like it is "another good man gone wrong." However, "Hans" is more than a lover. He has established a reputation as a serf, and is frequently visited by the "Chills" for advice, and they depart as wiser men. Let us not forget, however, that "Hans'" chief ambition was along military lines. Through persistent work, call it what you may, he found the Major out of a commission, and will win for himself the name of "Pilot." "Hans'" good nature and willingness to help has won for him many friends. These friends wish him the best life affords, and are confident of his success.

name: Arington, Brice Monroe
description: Southwest Virginia has contributed another type. He came in our four years ago, bringing with him a knowledge of horses and since has acquired a knowledge of women. Yes, he knows horses and women. What a "shark" he would have been in the days of horse and buggy when "Scotch" sold for fifty cents a quart. He is well known to all of us and is particularly well known to the so called weaker sex. Once more the clouds have drifted by and "God's gift to women" has assumed another form and face. But he is not always a lady's man. He never lets business interfere with pleasure but always manages to mingle in his classes. He is also one of our best baseball players. His active mental capacity, ready wit, and ability to make friends will carry him a long way in life. "Tech" wishes you success.

name: Beverley, Robert Carter
description: Carter Beverley of Richmond, a V. P. L. student—so was his old man. Mechanical Engineering is the career of course, the design of things, but of all the designs there is but one that catches his eye. This daringly designed thing moves of its own accord, where and when it pleases, and often against his wishes. An engineer not knowing the control of such perfectly designed things, and totally at loss as to what it will do—so was his old man, and your old man, and my old man. Of what use is Mechanical Engineering, anyway?

name: Hilisoly, Julius Philip
description: In 1921 "Billy" left behind the security of dear old Portsmouth to face the mysteries of Pre-Med at V.P.I. Considering the art of "curing 'em" for a time, he changed to Chemistry to study new ways of "killing 'em." Girls! Cupid got in his work early so that all through the years there has been only one girl—the "Only" one. "Billy" is never idle. When you cannot find him in his room working some chemical problem or writing letters to "her," you may be sure that he is at the Lyric. But above all, he is a good pal and can be depended on.

name: Cale, Edward Bettis
description: It was in the department of Agronomy that "Ed" chose to rest and refresh himself. It can be said that "Ed" took a great deal more than ordinary interest in his studies, and usually pulled down good marks for his pains. Ready and studious are the words that apply most forcibly to his character and habits, and such attributes will carry him a long way in his vocation. It seems natural for him to be quiet and to believe firmly that a "shut mouth" catches no flies and a still tongue makes a wise head. Even at that he has a way all of his own with the girls. And here the girls: "Ed" has a mighty good business head and should have no particular difficulty with that little problem of making two live as cheaply as one.

name: Letcher, Young Fields
description: He has made good, and he is a "star" in his classes. His injury has kept him out of many college activities. Despite his band cap, "L. Y." has shown himself a loyal Technian, a hard worker, and true friend. It is whispered that he gets letters daily from "a certain party" back in Grayson County. He is a gentleman in every sense of the word, and his list of friends and the class of '27 wish him much success in his chosen profession.

name: Gray, Edward Dohman
clubs: Freshman Track: Monogram Club: A. I. E. E.
Varsity Track: Monogram Club; Football Squad; A. I. E. E.
description: "Dot," unlike most of us, did not have to travel far to drink from the "Pierian Spring," for he is a native son of Blacksburg. "Dot" has always shown marked ability in the electrical line and has held the job as barracks electrician for several years, in which capacity he has shown himself industrious and self-reliant. He has always found time to take an active part in athletics and since his freshman year has earned his letter in track. He has always done his part for football by playing with the "sailors." Although his first love seems to have been radio, "Dot" finds time to give the ladies a treat now and then, and it is rumored that the dark eyes of a certain young lady have looked upon him with favor. With his pleasing personality, his friendly spirit, and love of fun he has made many friends here, and we predict for him a bright future after the parting which must come in June.

name: Waller, Staples Hunt
motto: "He knew what's what, and that's as high As metaphysic wit can fly."
description: Hunt came to T. P. L., not only with a world of experience, but also with a full conception of what really counts in life. He counts among his fortunate employers the N. & W. R. R. Co., N. Y. Edison Co., and General Electric Co. We can't say that he has been strong with the ladies; instead, we may say that he is strong for the lady, since he confines his attention to one girl and to his studies. He is currently far gone in love. Above all, though, his predominant interest is the pursuit of his chosen work. Hunt is a man with an able intellect, and we predict for him an important position in the electrical world. His ability must be recognized by the commercial world and it is only a question of time before he will be at the top of the ladder in his chosen profession.

name: Hux, William Jonathan Maiden's
description: "Hello, BILL!" A shout of welcome. Every member of the corps is glad to see "Bill" Hux, of Portsmouth. He's the life of the party wherever he goes. He has a "million dollar" personality that wins everyone. No wonder he is so popular, especially with the ladies. Like a magnet drawing steel, "BILL" draws the ladies. It must be a "gift of the gods" combined with his ability to dance. Hux is a convert of morphium and a great believer in the lines, "Oh sleep! It is a gentle thing, beloved from pole to pole." When it comes to work, "BILL" requires no tedious studying, no dreary mental exercise. It is all a matter of knowing how. We know that "BILL" will not allow his ability to become smothered and keep him chained to a low pay job, but place him on the highest rung of success in Civil Engineering.

name: Hottetl, James Johnson
description: After being bound with the shackles of militarism for a year, "J. Hottetl" became aware of the error of his ways and retired to a quiet and blissful married life. This bit of rare judgment indicates the high state of mental development attained by those pursuing the engineering profession. "J. Hottetl" has the appearance of a quiet fellow, but those who know him realize that his appearance is deceiving, for he bears a good argument. "J. Hottetl" is a hard worker and has had some good practical experience. Studies seem to be his favorite pastime and hobby. Knowing his natural ability in the field of work which he has chosen, we could predict nothing but success in his life's work. Those of us who know him admire his fine character and prize his friendship greatly. We hate to part with such a friend, and we shall not forget him.

name: Moore, John Carson
description: Nice John has been busy at Tech. He has shown us that a man can always find time to do something worth while even though his schedule is full. When he is not investigating how the milk weed will get along on the bottle, he is either planning a Beef and Horn Club program, or running down inhabitants of Montgomery County and teaching them points, ways, and methods of School lessons. John set out to master the secrets of Animal Husbandry and had made a pronounced success in this field. Not content with mastering one course, he decided to secure a broad education by also taking the course in Agricultural Education. John has a pleasing personality, is loyal to his friends, and commands the respect of his associates. With his high ideals and strong character his success is assured.

name: O'Sullivan, Samuel
motto: "work for what you get,"
clubs: Stock Judging Team at National Dairy Show, Indianapolis
description: This is the nature of the studious individual who came to our rescue in the fall of '23. "Sullie" began his military duties in Company "B," but about six weeks later, decided that there was more in college than military, and resigned from all military duties. His motto, "work for what you get," led him to the Dairy Department where he secured practical work along with theory. For four years he has fought a good fight and won his battle. "Sullie" is a man of few words, but when he speaks he always gives "food for thought." The interest "Sullie" has taken in dairying around Chicago leads us to believe that he may have other—other—other interests there. Here's to you, old boy; the class of '27 wishes you all the luck in the world. May Brandy, Virginia, send us more men to be as loyal a Technion as "Sullie."

name: Price, William Conway
description: Ladies and gentlemen, meet "Dean" Price, the smallest member of the class of '27, but in stature only, for as a student and leader he rules among the biggest men at Techland. "Dean" is a native of Blacksburg, being the oldest son of the Dean of Agriculture, H. L. Price. "Connie" probably wished to take engineering, but being too short to look through a transit, selected Horticulture instead. He was from the beginning the centre of the Horticulture class, and was soon selected as student adviser to his fellow students. Must any right one could find a crowd of students assembled in "Dean's" room seeking information, which he dished out with a smile. It would be hard to find a more practical, a more likable, or a more brilliant person than "Conway." Though we hate to lose this friend and loyal Techman, we are comforted to know the world will gain a man.
text: Ladies and gentlemen, meet "Dean" Price, the smallest member of the class of '27, but in stature only, for as a student and leader he rules among the biggest men at Techland. "Dean" is a native of Blacksburg, being the oldest son of the Dean of Agriculture, H. L. Price. "Connie" probably wished to take engineering, but being too short to look through a transit, selected Horticulture instead. He was from the beginning the centre of the Horticulture class, and was soon selected as student adviser to his fellow students. Must any right one could find a crowd of students assembled in "Dean's" room seeking information, which he dished out with a smile. It would be hard to find a more practical, a more likable, or a more brilliant person than "Conway." Though we hate to lose this friend and loyal Techman, we are comforted to know the world will gain a man.

name: Basche, Henry Richardson
description: The gentleman from Blackburg, the K. P. from Fort Monroe, and the life of any good party. What could be better than a party with "Boscoe" sticking in the "Sartane minor," or a dance with Henry to treat the ladies, or above all, a meal at camp with "Boscoe" officiating as K. P. He has the distinction of serving more K. P. duty than any other single man at Monroe last summer. While he did nothing "Boob," he had his fun and everybody liked him for it. "Boscoe" started in military with a gentleman's company, but during camp he developed a rowdy nature, and in order that this nature might be satisfied, he strayed as a "real gentleman" his last year. He can now run as far as Lynchburg and still doesn't have to cross the Chesapeake Bay. We understand that "Boscoe" intends to study Law next year. We believe he will make friends where he goes, and may his future classmates appreciate him as much as we have.

name: Woodward, Clifford Hawling
motto: "Music hath charms,"
description: but "Clifford" had not been in our midst long before the early morning music of the Bugle Corps ceased to hold any charms for him. Although a military man at the start, after answering "Here" a few mornings at reveille and standing a few inspections, he decided to give his desire for sleep full sway. He therefore joined the ranks of our "university" brethren. Clifford did not entirely desert us, for although he kept his trunk in town, he stayed in the barracks most of the time, where he was always ready for the inevitable hot air session. At one time Clifford must have owned a set of building blocks, since he tells us he will be a sculptor some day. Here at Tech, he has pursued the "green wiper" course, and just as he wins in this race by a nose, we are certain he will be victorious in life's great race, by virtue of the good qualities he possesses.
